essence: David designs a HIIT skipping routine (6x 1-min all-out bursts with 1.5-min rests, hung-on-door-handle rope, paired with park walks) and rejects a weekly sugar cheat day as a relapse trigger, committing instead to a 60-90 day abstinence window.

David decides OMI and Plaud are the same concept (voice-capture providers) so omi-fetch's name/skill split is wrong, and directs pulling raw audio/video locally for Whisper-or-Groq transcription instead of paying $20/month per provider, with a Claude pipeline doing tagging and ontology.

actions.text: Research a Claude-based transcription pipeline that ingests OMI/Plaud audio+video and runs tagging/ontology
Unify OMI and Plaud fetch under one provider-agnostic ingestion concept and rename omi-fetch accordingly

essence: David is angry that a Lisa/KDD learning ballooned into its own branch instead of one learning doc on the working ticket, and demands Lisa get a menuing/deterministic clarification step (ask the human, check one-off vs pattern, bump learning counts) so this class of mistake can't recur.

actions.text: Add deterministic menuing/clarification step to Lisa's workflow so she routes to the right path or asks the human
Enforce one-learning-document-per-ticket convention instead of spawning KDD branches

essence: Long triage session: David pins down what the KDD bridge actually is (a repo/MCP thrown out without visibility), decides Gemini is broken and must be deprecated out of the system, sets ticket flow (validate-then-close 176, open 192 in a new session, defer important-but-painful 191 until loose ends are cleaned), and frames the core problem as 'the factory writes knowledge but nothing reads it' — KDD docs should be read at PR-validation and knowledge-update time.

actions.text: Deprecate broken Gemini integration out of the system
Validate ticket 176 tests then close it; open 192 in a new session; defer 191 until loose ends are cleaned
Register the KDD bridge MCP in the application registry with clear purpose/ownership docs
Add last-pulled timestamp and new-data visibility to the OMI fetch app
Wire KDD document reading into structural workflow points (PR validation, knowledge update)

essence: About to be re-billed for Gling.ai he hasn't used in months, David commissions research to track down an agentic open-source video-cutting project surfaced in a recent conversation (possibly documented in the video-as-code brain / upstream repo), vet its code for prompt-injection/exfiltration safety, and find Gling/Descript replacements — cutting only, since Hyperframes will own editing/enhancement.

actions.text: Track down the prior video-editing conversation (video-as-code brain) and locate the agentic editing repo in upstream
Research safe open-source agentic alternatives to Gling and Descript for video cutting, vetting code quality and injection/exfiltration risk
Cancel/avoid Gling.ai subscription renewal before the upcoming charge

essence: Saturday Chiang Mai AI engineers meetup: Anthropic data-training/privacy drama (default data-sharing opt-in, Fable input/output recording, alleged region-tracking strings), Sonnet 5 vs GLM 5.2 cost/harness tradeoffs and cache-keepalive hacks, Artifacts rollout to subscription plans, Chinese open-source supply-chain exploit warnings, Spotify CTO's 4000-PRs/day talk vs Cursor's 'constrain the ideas' counterpoint; next Saturday cancelled for a US-embassy event, and David agrees to progress Angela and Stravan's onboarding using Angela's project documents.

actions.text: Send Angela's project documents through by email to progress her onboarding
Coordinate onboarding session with Angela and Stravan
